theme_and_archetype.e1_twist: Neo-Brutalism. Bold charcoal borders (1px to 2px solid), stark white cards on pastel backgrounds, and zero muddy drop-shadows. This grounds the softness of the pastels and prevents the app from feeling childish. It balances approachability with structural boldness.

headings.description: Geometric but soft sans-serif for massive display headings.

body.description: Highly readable, warm humanist sans for grid text and labels.

scale_hierarchy.h1: text-4xl sm:text-5xl lg:text-6xl tracking-tighter leading-none font-black
scale_hierarchy.h2: text-2xl sm:text-3xl lg:text-4xl tracking-tight leading-none font-bold

bento_grid.description: MODE B: THE CONTROL ROOM GRID for the dashboard, mixed with MODE A: THE TETRIS GRID for the landing page.
bento_grid.containers: Use Generous Spacing Philosophy. p-6 or p-8 inside cards with borders to prevent text collision.

surfaces.neo_brutalist_cards: bg-white border-2 border-slate-900 rounded-xl shadow-[4px_4px_0px_0px_rgba(15,23,42,1)] transition-transform hover:-translate-y-1 hover:shadow-[6px_6px_0px_0px_rgba(15,23,42,1)]

heatmap_grid.description: The core group schedule viewer. A grid where X = Days, Y = Hours (or vice versa).
heatmap_grid.styling: border-collapse. Every cell has a 1px solid border-slate-900. Color intensity dictates how many people are free.
heatmap_grid.interaction: Hovering over a cell reveals a tooltip (Shadcn tooltip) detailing EXACTLY who is free and who is busy (and why).
heatmap_grid.labels: Day names and Hour labels must be visible outside the grid (text-xs uppercase).

availability_editor.description: Individual view to toggle 1-hour slots. Supports clicking and dragging to select multiple slots.
availability_editor.styling: Clean toggles. Selected 'Free' blocks turn Mint. Selected 'Busy' blocks turn Red (or custom tagged color).

color_wheel_picker.description: A customized gradient/color picker component for users to define custom 'Busy Reasons'.
color_wheel_picker.implementation: Use HTML5 <input type='color'> styled heavily with Tailwind, or a custom HSL slider. Output a hex code applied to the busy reason tag.

marquee.description: Editorial Ribbon Marquee on the landing page showing names/avatars scrolling slowly.
marquee.implementation: react-fast-marquee with large gaps (gap-32) and pastel pill backgrounds.

images.description: Abstract 3D illustration of soft floating geometric shapes representing time and calendars. To be used as a visual element on the landing page.
Group of friends laughing, establishing a social, friendly vibe on the landing page.
Alternative friends image for bento grid layouts.

instructions_to_main_agent: DO NOT use Inter or Roboto. Inject the provided Google Fonts (Outfit and Work Sans) into the main HTML/CSS.
Implement the Neo-brutalist aesthetic: sharp 2px solid borders (slate-900) on cards, solid hard drop shadows, and soft pastel backgrounds. DO NOT USE blurry drop shadows.
For the heatmap: build a dynamic grid component. Ensure text labels (Days, Hours) are clear. Overlay cells with the appropriate heatmap intensity background. Add hover tooltips using Shadcn.
Ensure every interactive element has a data-testid (e.g., data-testid='create-group-btn', data-testid='heatmap-cell-monday-9am').
In the individual availability editor, allow users to set a custom color for a busy reason using an HSL/color picker input.
The Hero Section on the landing page should use the bento-grid layout, incorporating the hero_background image.
Mobile Responsiveness is critical. On mobile, the heatmap might need to be scrollable horizontally with a sticky leftmost column for hour labels.
